title: lepigonum purpureum
intro: Lepigonum purpureum, also known as the Purple Lepigonum, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae. It is a shrub native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea, known for its aromatic leaves and purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Lepigonum purpureum can be prepared as a herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and is often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive problems.
